title: Daily Market Digest: June 14, 2026
subtitle: Market closes higher driven by tech and SpaceX IPO, Fed signals hawkish shift.

items.headline: U.S. Stocks End Week Higher, Driven by Tech Enthusiasm and SpaceX Debut
items.summary: The U.S. stock market concluded the week on a positive note, with major indices extending gains as investors largely overlooked policy uncertainties and embraced risk-on assets. A significant catalyst was the highly anticipated public debut of SpaceX. However, the Federal Reserve's latest signals point to a potentially hawkish shift, with rate cuts appearing less imminent amid accelerating inflation. Economic data shows continued expansion, though energy costs and geopolitical tensions remain key concerns. Not financial advice.

items.headline: SpaceX Rockets on Debut
items.summary: SpaceX made a historic public debut on Nasdaq, with shares soaring and reaching a valuation above $2 trillion, marking the largest public offering in history.

items.headline: Inno Holdings Leads Monthly Gains
items.summary: Inno Holdings Inc. emerged as the top-performing stock in June with a significant monthly price appreciation.

items.headline: Roku Surges on Buyout Speculation
items.summary: Roku shares skyrocketed on Friday following market rumors of a potential media company buyout.

items.headline: Amer Oncology Network Shares Plummet
items.summary: American Oncology Network A saw a dramatic monthly decline, potentially linked to specific corporate developments or market re-evaluation.

items.headline: Hitek Global Drops After Offering
items.summary: Hitek Global Inc. experienced a sharp monthly fall in stock price following its announcement of an $8 million registered direct offering.

items.headline: Nike Dips Among Dow Losers
items.summary: Nike was among the biggest losers in the Dow Jones Industrial Average today.

items.headline: Fed Signals Hawkish Pivot Amid Accelerating Inflation
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's June FOMC meeting is anticipated to mark a significant policy shift, with the central bank expected to remove its easing bias. Accelerating core inflation, now running hotter than expected, and a stabilizing labor market are driving expectations for higher U.S. rates, potentially pushing rate cut forecasts into 2027.

items.headline: AI Policy and Spending Drive Tech Sector Focus
items.summary: Artificial intelligence continues to dominate market narratives, with an AI capital spending boom largely driving the S&P 500's year-to-date gains. Policy developments, including reports of the Trump administration moving to block foreign access to advanced AI models due to security concerns, highlight the increasing regulatory attention on the sector. Hyperscaler capital expenditure is projected to reach $720 billion in 2026.

items.headline: Energy Market Shifts as Solar Surpasses Coal in U.S.
items.summary: A significant macro signal emerged from the energy market, as solar power generated more electricity than coal in the U.S. for the first time in May. Solar supplied 12.8% of U.S. generation, topping coal's 12.2% share, despite ongoing government support for coal.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Expectations High, Healthcare Sector Lags
items.summary: S&P 500 companies posted strong Q1 2026 earnings growth of nearly 29%, with Q2 results potentially approaching 40%. However, the healthcare sector remains the lone contracting sector, with earnings expected to decline in Q2, while energy companies are forecasted for robust growth.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Continue to Impact Oil Prices
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical factors, particularly the war in Iran and challenges in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, continue to influence oil prices and contribute to inflationary pressures.
